title: Maxillaria standleyi
intro: Maxillaria standleyi, also known as Standley's Maxillaria, is a species of orchid native to the tropical regions of Central America. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its purported health benefits, including the treatment of respiratory issues, fever, and digestive problems. The bioactive constituents of Maxillaria standleyi include flavonoids, alkaloids, and glycosides, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties. Maxillaria standleyi is often used to make herbal teas and infusions, as well as tinctures and extracts, which are used to treat various health conditions, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness.
taxonomy: Maxillaria standleyi is a species of orchid that belongs to the family Orchidaceae. The genus Maxillaria is part of the subfamily Maxillarioideae, which is a group of orchids characterized by their long-lasting inflorescences. Maxillaria standleyi is native to Central America and is known for its unique flowers and growth habit. This species is often classified as a terrestrial orchid, meaning it grows in the ground rather than on other plants. Maxillaria standleyi is also a member of the subtribe Maxillariinae, which is a subgroup of the subfamily Maxillarioideae.
